content:
1180	  \textbf{Step~3: Contraction by extra degeneracy.} In the cover of $D(f)$, the member indexed by $f' = f$ is $D(f)$ itself. The augmented Čech complex therefore admits a section: map a family to its component at $f$. This section extends to an extra degeneracy on the augmented simplicial object, exhibiting the augmented Čech complex as contractible. Consequently the complex is exact in all positive degrees.
1181	
1182	  \textbf{Step~4: Globalization.} Each localized differential is exact, so the unlocalized differential is exact by the local-to-global principle for spanning sets. Hence $\check H^n(\mathfrak U, \mathcal O_X) = 0$ for $n \ge 1$.
1183	\end{proof}
1184	
1185	\begin{remark}[Finite-subspanning reduction]
1186	  \label{rem:basic_open_cech_finite_subspanning}
1187	  Although $s$ need not be finite, the unit ideal condition guarantees a finite subset $s_0 \subseteq s$ already generating the unit ideal. The two covers indexed by $s$ and $s_0$ induce the same sieve on $U$, so the refinement map is a quasi-isomorphism. It therefore suffices to prove acyclicity for a finite cover, which makes every cochain factor a finite product.
1188	\end{remark}
1189	
1190	\begin{remark}[Lean implementation]
1191	  The proof uses the local-to-global exactness theorem for spanning sets, the structure-sheaf-section-as-localization theorem for affine basic opens, exactness preservation under localization, and the extra-degeneracy contraction theorem for augmented simplicial objects. Mathlib does not currently host a formalisation of the Koszul complex or of the Čech-to-derived-functor spectral sequence; the proof above bypasses both by working directly with the augmented complex on each basic-open patch.
1192	\end{remark}
1193	
1194	\begin{remark}[Implementation status (iter-108 escape-valve)]
1195	  \label{rem:basicOpenCover_step2_status}
1196	  The Lean implementation of Step~2 lands inline at \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean}, lines~$1781$–$1846$, inside the body of the per-generator block \texttt{h\_loc\_exact}. Substeps~(i)–(iii) of the Mathlib-API recipe above — the image-Finset bridge of substep~(i), the restriction-of-section identity $V_x \cap D(f) = D(f|_{V_x})$ of substep~(ii), and the per-coord \texttt{IsLocalization.Away} certificate via \texttt{IsAffineOpen.isLocalization\_of\_eq\_basicOpen} of substep~(iii) — are committed as inline \texttt{have}-declarations at lines~$1786$–$1834$, together with the auxiliary inclusion $V_x \subseteq U$ that feeds substep~(iii) (approximately fifty lines of inline scaffolding, accumulated across iter-108 and iter-109 partial scaffolding).
1197	
1198	  The trailing transport — substep~(iv) of the recipe (per-$x$ \texttt{Algebra} and \texttt{IsScalarTower} synthesis, the \texttt{IsLocalizedModule.pi} lift on the cochain-degree product, with \texttt{IsLocalizedModule.prodMap} furnishing the binary-product specialisation) together with the ladder-\texttt{LinearEquiv} transport of $h_{a_0\text{-fun}}(f)$ — is \emph{deferred at iter-108 (Archon canonical iter-108) as a budget deferral, not a Mathlib gap.} Mathlib at the project's pin \texttt{b80f227} already contains \texttt{IsLocalizedModule.Away}, \texttt{IsLocalizedModule.pi}, \texttt{IsLocalizedModule.prodMap}, and the algebra-map$\to$localized-module adapter \texttt{instIsLocalizedModuleTo\-LinearMap\-To\-AlgHom\-Of\-IsLocalization\-AlgebraMap\-Submonoid}; the lemma is mechanizable from these pieces. The mechanization was deferred at iter-108 because the per-$x$ typeclass plumbing (in particular, threading \texttt{letI \dots in $\langle$goal-type$\rangle$} through the Lean elaborator) is eager rather than body-binder-introducing, so the per-$x$ algebra setup has no surviving binder to attach to; rewriting the block to introduce the typeclasses via a body-level \texttt{let} or a separate auxiliary lemma is a sizeable proof-engineering exercise that has been parked behind higher-priority Phase~B objectives and the C1 promotion of the refined \texttt{LineBundle} carrier. The corresponding inline \texttt{sorry} at \texttt{BasicOpenCech.lean:$1846$} is annotated with a budget-deferral marker (\texttt{DEFERRED (budget)}); it is not flagged as a \texttt{MATHLIB GAP}, and consequently does not enter the project's enumerated Mathlib-gap list. As of iter-110 that named-deferral surface comprises six entries — \texttt{instIsMonoidal\_W} (varying-ring \texttt{stalk\_tensorObj} gap; \texttt{Modules/Monoidal.lean:$173$}), \texttt{cotangentExactSeq\_structure.h\_exact} (sheaf-of-modules exactness criterion; \texttt{Differentials.lean:$636$}), \texttt{nonempty\_jacobianWitness} (Hilbert/Quot schemes plus finite-group quotients; \texttt{Jacobian.lean:$179$}), \texttt{PicardFunctor.representable} (gated on Phase~C3; \texttt{Picard/Functor.lean:$181$}), \texttt{SheafOfModules.pullback\_tensorObj} (the $\mu$-iso of the missing varying-ring \texttt{Monoidal} instance; \texttt{Picard/LineBundle.lean:$82$}), and \texttt{SheafOfModules.pullback\_oneIso} (the $\epsilon$-iso of the same; \texttt{Picard/LineBundle.lean:$96$}) — together with the present budget-deferral at \texttt{BasicOpenCech.lean:$1846$}, which sits outside that surface.
1199	\end{remark}
1200	
1201	\subsection{Use in the project}
1202	
1203	The chain built in this chapter composes as follows. Čech acyclicity for the structure sheaf on basic-open covers yields the Čech-acyclic hypothesis. The comparison isomorphism between Čech and derived cohomology (a project-local construction, queued for a subsequent iteration) yields the $\HasCechToHModuleIso$ hypothesis. The existence producer of Theorem~\ref{thm:Scheme_hasAffineCechAcyclicCover_of_basicOpen} then assembles these into $\HasAffineCechAcyclicCover$, which the producer of Theorem~\ref{thm:Scheme_instIsAffineHModuleVanishing_of_hasAffineCechAcyclicCover} converts into $\IsAffineHModuleVanishing$. Together with the $H^0$ finiteness ladder of Chapter~\ref{chap:Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK}, this closes Phase~A step~6 and delivers the genus carrier $\Module.\Finite\,k\,H^1(C, \mathcal O_C)$.
1204	
1205	\paragraph{Status (iter-108 / Archon canonical iter-108).}
1206	The substantive theorem~\ref{thm:basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf} currently rests on six labelled transient \texttt{sorry}s inside its Lean proof body: a PAUSED \texttt{cechCofaceMap\_pi\_smul} bridge at \texttt{BasicOpenCech.lean:$1120$}; substep~(a) of the augmented Čech reduction at line~$1212$; the outer transport $K \to K_0$ at line~$1536$; substep~(a) for the finite-subspanning subset $s_0$ at line~$1564$; the budget-deferred Step~2 transport at line~$1846$ (per Remark~\ref{rem:basicOpenCover_step2_status}); and a downstream substep at line~$1754$ that is gated on closure of the line-$1120$ bridge. The file compiles end-to-end against these labelled sorries, and the chain into $\IsAffineHModuleVanishing$ and the downstream Phase~A step~$6$ consumer flows through unchanged.
1207	
1208

215	
216	\section{Curves and the canonical sheaf}
217	
218	The case of a smooth proper curve $C \to \Spec k$ is the special case $n = 1$: the relative cotangent sheaf $\Omega_{C/k}$ is a line bundle on $C$, classically denoted $\omega_C$ and called the \emph{canonical sheaf}.
219	
220	\begin{theorem}\leanok
221	[Serre-duality genus equality]
222	  \label{thm:serre_duality_genus}
223	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.serre_duality_genus}
224	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_sheaf}
225	  Let $C$ be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field $k$. Then
226	  \[
227	    \dim_k H^0(C, \Omega_{C/k}) \;=\; \dim_k H^1(C, \struct{C}).
228	  \]
229	  Both sides equal the genus $g(C)$. The equality is the dimension one case of Serre duality applied to the canonical sheaf; combined with the definition $g(C) = \dim_k H^1(C, \struct{C})$, this provides the alternative differential-theoretic characterisation used when computing the relative dimension of $\Jac(C)$ at the identity.
230	\end{theorem}
231
Now I'll make the edit. I'll go with topical order — placing `serre_duality_genus` right after `cotangentExactSeq_structure.h_exact` since both live in `Differentials.lean`.

# Blueprint Writer Report — mv-iter112

## Slug
mv-iter112

## Iteration
112

## Status
COMPLETE

## Summary

Updated the `\begin{remark}[Implementation status (iter-108 escape-valve)]` block at `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex:1194-1199` (label `rem:basicOpenCover_step2_status`) to reflect the iter-110 reclassification of `serre_duality_genus` into the named-deferral surface.

## Changes applied

Single-line LaTeX edit in `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex` (within line 1198 of the remark body):

1. **Count**: `"comprises six entries"` → `"comprises seven entries"`.
2. **Temporal qualifier**: `"As of iter-110"` → `"As of iter-112"`.
3. **Entry insertion**: added `\texttt{serre\_duality\_genus}` (Serre duality for smooth proper curves; `\texttt{Differentials.lean:$877$}`) to the enumeration.

## Insertion location decision

Per the directive's two acceptable orderings (topical vs. chronological), I chose **topical**: `serre_duality_genus` is inserted immediately after `cotangentExactSeq_structure.h_exact`, grouping the two `Differentials.lean` entries (`:636` and `:877`) adjacent to each other. Rationale: a reader scanning the file/location field benefits from co-located entries by source file, and the two Differentials gaps form a coherent topical pair (cotangent exactness criterion + Serre duality on the canonical sheaf).

## Final enumeration order in remark

1. `instIsMonoidal_W` — `Modules/Monoidal.lean:173`
2. `cotangentExactSeq_structure.h_exact` — `Differentials.lean:636`
3. `serre_duality_genus` — `Differentials.lean:877` *(new)*
4. `nonempty_jacobianWitness` — `Jacobian.lean:179`
5. `PicardFunctor.representable` — `Picard/Functor.lean:181`
6. `SheafOfModules.pullback_tensorObj` — `Picard/LineBundle.lean:82`
7. `SheafOfModules.pullback_oneIso` — `Picard/LineBundle.lean:96`

Together with the budget-deferral at `BasicOpenCech.lean:1846`, which the remark continues to distinguish as outside the named-deferral surface.

## Scope discipline

- No other chapters touched.
- No Lean source edits.
- No new theorem/lemma blocks introduced.
- No `\leanok` / `\mathlibok` markers added (this is informational prose).
- Substep numbering (i)–(iv) in the prior paragraph untouched.
- No strategy change proposed; STRATEGY.md remains authoritative.

## LOC delta

~3 LOC delta (single sentence rewritten within a single remark body); well under the <20 LOC budget mentioned in the directive.

## Verification

- The 7-entry list in the remark now matches the named-gap roster verified by `blueprint-reviewer-iter112` and recorded in `STRATEGY.md`.
- The Serre-duality entry's description ("Serre duality for smooth proper curves") aligns with the theorem statement at `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex:220-230` (`thm:serre_duality_genus`).
- The temporal qualifier "As of iter-112" is now consistent with the current iteration and resolves the iter-110 self-contradiction flagged in the directive.

## Outputs

- Modified: `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex` (remark block at lines `1194-1199`).
- Report: this file.
